text.source_text: यत्पुनरुक्तं प्रियसंसूचितोपक्रमाद्विज्ञानात्मन एवायं दर्शनाद्युपदेश इति , अत्र ब्रूमः —
text.transliteration_iast: yatpunaruktaṃ priyasaṃsūcitopakramādvijñānātmana evāyaṃ darśanādyupadeśa iti , atra brūmaḥ —

translation.literal_translation: As for what was said - that because of the opening marked by dearness, the injunction to see and hear concerns only the empirical conscious self - to that we reply as follows.
translation.technical_translation: Transition into Ashmarathya rebuttal: restates the priya-upakrama objection before replacing it with a pratijna-siddhi-based reading.
translation.interpretive_note: Formal objection restatement preceding doctrinal correction.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: This is a brief pivot line; style may be merged with neighboring record in final polish.
Term choice 'empirical conscious self' can be normalized with your fixed vijñanatman vocabulary.
